name: tshark
title: tshark
description:
network traffic analyzer - console version
Wireshark is a network "sniffer" - a tool that captures and analyzes
packets off the wire. Wireshark can decode too many protocols to list
here.
.
This package provides the console version of wireshark, named
"tshark".

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tshark because network traffic analyzer - console version.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use tshark as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tshark  [ -i <capture interface>|- ] [ -f <capture filter> ] [ -2 ] [ -r <infile> ] [ -w <out-` as documented upstream for tshark.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tshark -h|--help` as documented upstream for tshark.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tshark -v|--version` as documented upstream for tshark.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tshark with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tshark --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: tshark - Dump and analyze network traffic
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install wireshark. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `tshark  [ -i <capture interface>|- ] [ -f <capture filter> ] [ -2 ] [ -r <infile> ] [ -w <out-`. Further invocations from upstream: `tshark -h|--help`; `tshark -v|--version`. network traffic analyzer - console version.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: network traffic analyzer - console version.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: tshark - Dump and analyze network traffic.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install wireshark
